items.caption:
Doing THIS will be the reason why you get ahead! 

Think about it. When did you ever really do it all alone? 

It’s rare because the best projects are a TEAM EFFORT.

And you can lead that charge by creating a Shared PDF Space in @adobeacrobat!

So the next time you’re owning a big project, upload all your docs and notes, and then give your team access to add their own!

When it’s time for the final recommendation, AI Assistant can take in all the feedback and notes, and help you give your best stuff to your boss.

Now that’s the way to do it! 

Check out Acrobat PDF Spaces! 

#AdobeAcrobatPartner #Career
items.transcript: I'm going to tell you something your boss won't. If you want to get ahead in your career, you've got to be the playmaker on your team. Not just the one who puts their heads down and works in a silo, but the one who makes decisions off of everyone's inputs and connects them all together. Let's say you're building a new product roadmap. Back in the day, you'd be done with sharing hundreds of emails and sharing notes, but today you can do it all in one place, and I'm partnering with Adobe Acrobat to show you how. And you start by creating a shared PDF space in Adobe Acrobat. This is where you upload your strategy deck, meeting notes, and input from your team. Then you'll have it all in one spot, ready to be dissected and analyzed. Share this access to everyone on your team and let them add their notes to keep things streamlined. They won't see your chat history, but it's a new, improved shared working space for teams. Now here's where you really make your mark, because you can then ask an assistant specific questions to help build your project recommendations. Like, what are some key learnings that could be implemented in future projects? And show me the data points to support our new product. An AI assistant only pulls its answers from the relevant information in your documents. You can even click the citation to show exactly where it gathered the information or save a response as a note if you want to share with your full team reference. So now when you present it to your boss, you're not just an individual contributor, you're a playmaker connecting the whole team together. Try out shared PDF spaces in Adobe Acrobat and be the one on your team who puts it all together. Check out the LinkedIn profile. Good luck!

Your job is KILLING you!

Comment SUMMER to join our free job search mastery classes and preorder our book!

Your job sucks and I want you to do something about it, my friend. 

If you got the Sunday scaries right now…. watch this and see which one applies. 

And to learn the real remedy to get out of a job and into a job you love, comment SUMMER to preorder our Get Hired book and join all our free bonus classes!

Intro by alexandraromanello

#career #newjob #sundayscaries
items.transcript: The biggest sign that you're in the wrong job is when you think about your job and see how your body reacts to it. Because there's actually truth behind the Sunday Scaries. If you're already dreading the week ahead and your body closes up and you don't want to do anything and anxiety takes over, you're in the wrong job. What I found after coaching thousands of people with WorkApp, there's really only three criteria that you need to hit off to enjoy the work you do. The first one is, do I even like the job that I'm doing? Not if I'm good at it, but if I like it. The second one is, do I like the people that I'm doing with? So this is your boss, their boss, your team members, all of it. And the third thing is, do I like the life that it gives me? So money, work-life balance, your vacation, do you like that? If the answer is a yes, yes, yes, I will guarantee you that your body will notice a difference. But if there's a gap, if maybe you don't like the job, but you like the people, and you don't like the work-life balance and the pay, then something needs to give. You need to find a different role, maybe at the same company, and get that pay bump. But if all three are X's, then your body is going to tell you, and you're not going to want to do anything. So it's Sunday right now, I want you to think about it for yourself. How do you feel when it comes to work, and how does your body feel about it? That's the real answer if you should get a new job or not. Good luck.

items.caption:
I know things feel scary right now… but you’re not alone, my friend.

The data shows that there is more uncertainty than ever before.​

- Korn Ferry found that as many as 70% of positions are filled internally or by referrals​

- That a traditional resume only accounts for about 10% of what gets someone a job​

- And nearly 60% of of employees believe that AI could replace their role in 3 years…​

But now is not the time to panic! 

It’s time to assess how things have changed and where they are going.​

We are in this together and can figure it out with great resources like @kornferry by our side.​

What’s been the most challenging part of your career journey so far?​

#kornferrypartner
items.transcript: Tell me why all job search and career advice sounds like this right now. Take initiative. Don't do that without asking first. You gotta use AI to work smarter. Nah, that work feels too AI. To get ahead, we need specialists. Wait, why aren't you more cross-functional? You know why it feels like that? Because we're in one of the weirdest times we've ever had in job market history. Cornferry found that as many as 70% of positions are filled internally or by a referral. That a traditional resume only accounts for about 10% of someone getting a job. And a whopping 60% of employees think that AI could replace a role in three years. So yes, I know it's scary out there. But the best thing we could do right now is understand the old way of working is not effective anymore. The days of climbing the corporate ladder, staying in one role forever, sucking up to your boss, those days are gone, my friend. Now it's really about adapting your role to match with the business needs, emphasizing on the human side that AI can't take away, and really having options in your career. As Cornferry tells us, your career durability comes down to these two things. One, knowing your unique strengths that no one else can take away. And two, you evolving them as the business evolves as well. So if you're feeling lost in your career right now, my friend, just know you are not alone. Everybody is feeling it and you're going to figure this out like you always do. Keep going.

items.caption:
As a millennial manager… this is what I’ve found work with some of my Gen Z team members 😂 

But I am no expert. I am still learning shit on the fly!

Gen Z been getting a lot of flack on the internet but I think they are the first generation to realize the game is rigged. And you know what… more power to them!

What are your best tips for a new manager?

#career #genz #manager #millennial
items.transcript: As a millennial who's hired a lot of Gen Z, I think I've cracked the code of how to work with them and how to get the best out of them. Gen Z don't like surprises, so you can't just message them this out of the blue or this out of the blue. You need to let them know if you do need a call that there's no danger coming. So say something like this. And I don't care how young you are as a millennial, Gen Z is not gonna want to hang out with you, so you need to surround them with other Gen Z. Because if your office looks like this, I guarantee you your Gen Z is not gonna last and they're gonna be looking elsewhere from day one. And the last tip I'll give for millennial managers, you can't expect your Gen Z staff to be like how you were or how you are right now. I think as millennials, we're fed this whole stay late, come early, put your head down, don't talk back. Gen Z is not like that. They're gonna do all those things that we were taught not to do. But on the flip side, they're also gonna give you creativity and outside the box thinking, new slangs and fresh energy. They're gonna give you shit that we were never also given too. The best way to get your Gen Z staff energized, excited about work, you gotta meet them where they're at. You can't expect them to change and don't give them panic attacks with this, okay?

items.caption:
If you ever get put on a PIP… watch this!

Because I’m telling you from my HR experience, it is soooo incredibly rare to come out of a PIP with actual improved performance. 

If your manager is good and genuine about wanting to help you, by all means work your ass!

But if they just want you out, it don’t matter how hard you work! They still won’t want you 😤 

What would you do in this situation?

Intro by @ roberthatis

P.S. Want to pivot to a new job this year? Comment PIVOT to join our free career pivot masterclass starting on Tuesday!

#career #pivot #newjob #pip
items.transcript: I know pips suck, but the last thing you want to do if you get put on one is to fight it. Instead, you want to do this. So for OP's case, he had two options. Tell the manager why he's wrong, go to HR, but there's a secret third option that he hasn't considered. And the third option, and what I recommend for most people, don't do anything. Instead, focus all your time and energy into an exit plan. Because most times, a PIP is not a performance improvement plan. A PIP is a plan to fire you and just finding a way to do that. Unless you absolutely love this job and you know that you're destined to be in it and improve, then yes, stay there, work your ass off, maybe even talk to a senior team member who can help vouch for you. But from my HR experience, it's so hard to overcome a PIP. So I'd rather you look for something else and then focus your energy and effort on that. Okay, my friend? Good luck.

items.caption:

This is the real hack to double your salary!

Because the real gap between $75K and $150K isn’t effort. It’s strategy and asking for it in the right way.

There are 3 things that actually separate the people who get paid from the people who get passed over — I call them the 3 Ps.

Being proactive + aligning with business priorities + staying promotion ready.

And you can showcase all of that with @thequillbot!

And you can be the person who walks into their performance reviews with receipts and a brag book that documents their impact, ties it to business goals, and makes the case for what’s next.

Build yours in minutes with QuillBot for free! 

#QuillBot #QuillBotEducator #ad
items.transcript: The difference between you earning 75K and 150K comes down to these three things. And no, it's not effort or staying late at the office. I call it the three P's. And the three P's are being proactive, aligning with business priorities, and staying promotion ready. Because trust me, I know we've all been here before. All right, it's your performance review. Let's get into your goals. Goals? Oh, of course. Here's a list of our big wins for the quarter, how my role impacts business objectives, and next steps for my role. Studies show that people who get paid don't wait for their opportunity, they create it, and you can do that with Quilvok like this. Say you have a one-to-one review with your boss, go to Quilvok beforehand and enter this prompt. I have an upcoming performance review with my boss. Create me a brag book that includes all my big wins and how they relate back to business objectives. And just like that, you got the running doc to create a document that's going to get you paid. So if this is your year to get promoted, get paid, and get recognized, check out Quilvok and check it out for free. Do it!

items.caption:
Born to align these boxes and adjust the font 😤 

😂 The best thing I got out of my consulting days is my powerpoint skills baby. 

Where all my consultants at?

#corporatehumor #consulting

The sweet spot in corporate is being an IC 👀 

#career #work #manager
items.transcript: After being in HR for 10 years, I'm going to tell you this. The sweetest spot in corporate that you can find is being a highly paid individual contributor. Yep, I agree. I think being a specialized IC is better than any management job. Let's talk about it. Back in the day, being management was the thing that everyone wanted. You get paid more, you get these perks, you get the title, but now with all of these layoffs happening, people realize there's no teams that are safe right now. But you know what is safe? It's being the specialized individual who can only do that one thing at the company that no one else can do. A mistake that a lot of companies make is try to turn the specialized IC into a manager with a new title, new money, new promotion, but that also comes with more responsibilities, less time to do your IC stuff, less specialist and more of a generalist. So I say if you're in this specialized IC role and then they offer you this promotion, deny it, my friend, because ultimately doing this is going to be more lucrative. If you had a choice between being this or this, which one do you choose? Good luck.

items.caption:
That’s a no for me dawg 😂

Don’t let companies sweet talk you into giving LABOR FOR FREE!

You got skills, you got value. And companies should be paying you for that, my friend. You are valued!

Filmed at my fave coworking spot @thehivehongkong 🙌
